comments.body: Note: The static-build failure will be dealt with in a separate PR

@edsantiago I had some time waiting for other tests to run so I threw this together, incorporating some of your expressed concerns over `/etc/environment`.  Hopefully you'll agree this is a much more POLA and robust way of doing things.

> I really dislike the use of a /etc file for something that should only be necessary for rootless tests

This change isn't just for rootless tests, it's for ALL tests.  It's suppose to do what it says on the tin:  Consolidate all control variables into one-place for simplicity and debug-ability.  This way nobody ever has to guess where these are defined on the system for any given context.  It also makes logging them easy, separate from the "current" env. vars. printout.

> It's suppose to do what it says on the tin

Oh, woops.  I thought I had filled out the commit message more than that...
Hey! tests all passed in a single go (no re-runs).  Addressed all of @edsantiago concerns.
